## [00:00 - 03:00]

This is Deepesh and welcome to the BPM method training program, which is all around giving you stability predictability and Profitability now in this module. I want to take you through what the BPM method actually is It's a short video to give you an insight into what I've created and why so I'll be covering the definition of the BPM method Why the BPM method is relevant to you right now as an advertiser and how it will continue to pay back for Many many years to come as well for you personally and how it remains so perfectly in tune with Facebook ads Despite all the changes I've seen over the last five six years It still works and then I'll give you an insight into the core model That is the BPM method and what to expect as part of the training program as well So the brand driven performance marketing method is a mixture of brand marketing performance marketing and advertising Now why I want you to understand from this is when I talk about branding. I'm not talking about logos colors Copy guidelines and things like that which many people confuse That you know, that's all branding is all about but I've worked with many many big players big hitters from agency side In-house over the last 15 and more years and I really got a big appreciation as to what branding really is about now I'll be honest when I started off in my marketing. I went straight into performance marketing now the definition of a performance marketer is someone who is looking to Put money in and get money out essentially, but it's very much data driven So the best performance marketers out there are they're quite technical their data driven results orientated It's it's it's very much more left brain than brand marketing, which is very much more right brain Now it's not not quite this simple split between that but I came into marketing as a performance marketer So I was really driven by the numbers and and everything made sense to me I could have a hundred people hit my site maybe 5% converted which means five people actually bought and then I could look at all the metrics in between that I could look at Average order values and I got really caught up into the world of performance marketing and actually the first AB test split test that I ran Really helped me to better understand brand marketing now that the story there was we had installed a new split test tool around 2006 2007 and The recommendation from the expires hours was working a real simple test on a sign-up page And we'll test your brand green button brand branded green versus a red which is stock contrast We ran the test for two weeks and the numbers said that the red button

## [03:01 - 06:02]

Performed I think it was about 30% better in converting to brochure sign-ups Than the green button so my performance marketing head said hey, this is awesome We just literally the first test we've launched We got 30% more sign-ups on the landing page than using a green button So obviously logically you go back to the marketing director and say run this test. It's amazing I've got 30% increase in conversion all we did was to change the button from green to red and Then the brand marketing Manager said that's never gonna happen because it's not within our brand guidelines and at the time I was too inexperienced to understand and Because I was fairly junior. I kind of just went well But the numbers and they said yes, but it's not within our brand guidelines So there's no chance of using a red button instead of green and I was so deflated I kind of walked away from that thinking Wow, did that really happen? But the numbers say this and as I got deeper into the world of branding I understood What branding actually meant so, you know if if if I was given full control over the website The website would probably not look as branded as it should have so we would I would have gone We've all worked with the numbers lead I didn't really care about branding at the time But there has to be a balance because if you're creating Something short-term and for profit and you know, you just want as much revenue You don't really care about nurturing customers in building a business You'll probably go down the route that I started to go down Which is trusting the numbers and just performing everything around the numbers But branding is about building a long-term relationship with your customer Now the part of branding that I really took out into the BPM method was all about the consumer The everything starts with a consumer if you think about your product or service There's either a need that they absolutely need this thing or there's a desire that they want this thing What I try and pull out into the BPM method is to Communicate that in a way as an advertiser that just makes sense for your user to get them into your funnel And then you can convert them So that's what brand marketing and performance marketing are about when it comes to the BPM method and then it all plows in To creating advertising campaigns that just absolutely work and this is really what the BPM method is about is Constructing your avatar Understanding where your product fits building copy and creatives around that and delivering your Advertising in a performance marketing way. So you're measuring how your your campaigns are performing and analyzing and tweaking etc But you're doing it in a way that creates consistency and predictability What I've done for this actual training program is to split out principles Strategies and tactics the principles I use in the BPM method are Principles that have been around longer than I've been advertising on Facebook longer than Facebook's been around longer than the

## [09:03 - 12:05]

2014-2015 where you could advertise and to an extent almost sell anything without trying too hard And there's a reason for that number one because Facebook was a fairly new platform for Commerce so if they weren't as many advertisers It was still a novel thing to see a product in your newsfeed click it and buy a product or service from there So a lot of people were willing to spend money and also people were introducing new services that you couldn't necessarily buy online previously as well. So if you think about 2014 and print-on-demand t-shirt sellers that were making tons and tons of Dollars off the platform because print-on-demand and buying online It was still a novelty and I still kick myself that I didn't jump on that trains sooner because You know that that was a time to really get in and really scale up because your advertisers your your customers were exactly like this They were literally throwing money at you Where we are right now is the problem because now on Facebook there are too many ads in your newsfeed too many Competitors in your niche and it's getting harder and harder to cut through So what ends up happening is your consumers are actually not so sure whether they want to transact with you whether to sign up for your service or buy your product and that's what we're trying to break out of with the BPM method to take consumers that are Doubtful or concerned or not convinced and turn them into customers that are ready to pay and buy your product or service Once you've been through the BPM method my absolute goal is that you can look at this and turn around What what are your concerns into benefits? So forget about higher costs? It's about how affordable Facebook hands is for you right now Don't worry about competition focus on stability and how well it's performing for you day in day out Forget fast and Facebook potentially dying create profitable ad campaigns that work for you consistently right now Don't worry about the algorithm changes. We we will create something that's scalable by following these systems and methodologies Don't worry about worrying about all the different ad products focus on which ones are going to make a difference to you Don't don't spend too much time worrying about hacks and new ways of Kind of all these new tactics that come out focus on structured growth I have I've spoken to past students who Within the fun kind of first three to four months. They try them out. It doesn't work their performance is all over the place You get instability because people are doing fast scaling and manual bidding and things like that And they come back to the BPM method Calm down a bit take a deep breath and start going through the program again Because when you follow the systemized process it just works and the whole thing becomes a lot more predictable

## [12:06 - 15:06]

Because one of the core things about this is there are three players when it comes to advertising There's you as an advertiser with your goals There's the user that you're targeting with their own goals and then there's the advertising platform i.e. in this case Facebook Now going deeper into the world of Facebook this may or may not be a graphic that you've seen before this graphic is How the Facebook algorithm works and we'll cover that off later in the program But the key thing I want you to understand about this is this algorithm hasn't changed much over the year over the years But the BPM method still continues to work and this quote that Facebook shared about three four years ago in one of their presentations Really encompasses why the BPM method is so powerful So if I read this quote to you our goal and this is Facebook our goal is to match the right ad To the right person at the right time So just think about that if you're reading that and you're thinking yeah, absolutely, you know me as an advertiser That's exactly why one then great. You're ahead of 80% of advertisers out there, but if you're looking at that and thinking yeah, I'm not too sure If your goal is not to match the right ad for the right person at the right time you're doing something wrong That's a big part of how we build funnels and creatives and targeting in the BPM method that you're gonna see and We try and build Aligned to what Facebook want then the second part of this which is really quite interesting is Facebook say this is different Then a traditional auction because the winner isn't the ad with the highest monetary bid i.e. eBay But the ad that creates the most total value That's a big reason why the BPM method allows me to run my ads with consistency with predictability But also allows me to not worry about things like manual bidding manual bidding something will cover off later in the program But essentially manual bidding allows you to override the auction So if your ad gets presented to a user what manual bid does is says if my total value isn't good enough to win this particular auction Artificially bid it up. So you end up paying more sometimes it works. Sometimes it doesn't but why do you need to do that? It's because something in the Facebook algorithm Has given you a lower score. So looking at this you've got advertiser bid on the left You've got estimated action rate in the middle and then user value The BPM method is all about maximizing all of those and when you go through the program And you really get your ads honed in and you're targeting and your funnels and load time and everything else around that then your campaigns are far more effective So just to close out the introduction to the BPM method This is now the core model within the BPM method engine and the split between brand marketing mastery and

Everything falls out the back of that the 3n ads formula, which is how to create cut-through ads so that you're targeting people that are going to Click into your site, but you don't want to also target people that are not going to Convert so for example a good ad for me is not one that creates maximum click-through rate But it creates the right click-through rate bringing the right people into my funnel and then into the full funnel profit system Which goes back to what Facebook one is being able to serve the right ad to the right person at the right time So there's the kind of key elements of the brand part of brand performance marketing Then we go into perform marketing and this is when we get into Building the kind of foundations to create high predictability and reduce anxiety Through the predictability path so it's about how we structure our campaigns how we do our monitoring and our insights and keep control of our Ad accounts without our ad accounts controlling us That's a big part of the BPM method because I want you at the end of this program to feel less anxiety And be in control of your results and then the graduation framework where we go through fast Testing iterative testing to maximize your outcomes for the minimal amount of spend and time and then the profitability Optimization which is all about balancing your ad costs with conversion rate and revenue per user They're three big parts of profitability with Facebook ads Which is you want to pay less you want to convert more and generate higher revenue per user?
